_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Repairing machines or systems using the needed tools.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in monitoring data pipeline execution, identifying failure patterns, and implementing corrective actions that address root causes rather than symptoms.
comments: Functional overlap: repairing data systems after failures. WIDAI covers corrective action and remediation but 'repairing' as a physical skill doesn't directly translate. The functional outcome — restoring systems to working state — intersects.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Weak similarity (nist band: much more dissimilar than similar). The low STS score indicates a narrow intersection. The conceptual overlap exists but is limited — most of each description's semantic content is non-overlapping.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8621 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision exceeds Recall, indicating that the FDE's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse — the KSA contains concepts or specificity beyond what the FDE covers. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (99.62%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is the expected NLI outcome for Intersects relationships — the descriptions share conceptual territory without one implying the other.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.3931) meets or exceeds the STS cross-encoder (0.1802). This is uncommon and suggests the semantic similarity is well-represented in the embedding space — the descriptions occupy similar vector-space regions even without joint processing.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Minimal lexical overlap (0.00% of tokens shared). Low lexical overlap combined with low STS indicates both surface vocabulary and underlying semantics diverge between these descriptions.
